description: Lock Continuous Integration to frequent shared-mainline integration verified by automated builds and tests, separate from open-source licensing and cloud hosting.

checks.reason: CI remains a software-engineering practice, not an infrastructure product alone.
The scoped practice requires a shared version-controlled mainline or controlled source repository.
CI needs version control as the mainline/repository mechanism for frequent integration.
The scoped practice requires automated build and test execution to verify each integration.
Automated build/test verification is core CI method, not optional scaling context.
Open-source licensing and community workflow are not direct prerequisites for CI.
Cloud service delivery can host CI later, but CI does not require cloud infrastructure as its core prerequisite.
The node keeps its coarse 1980s antecedent anchor while documenting date uncertainty.
